title: execute_bash: boolean flag parameter 'run_in_background' violates split-function rule
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Structural Modularity (Boolean Flag Parameter)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/bash/mod.rs lines 309-389 (execute_bash), 314-327 (run_in_background branch)

**Violation:**
execute_bash receives a run_in_background: bool inside args, then forks into two completely different execution semantics at lines 320-327 vs 328-388 — different return contracts (shell_id vs stdout/stderr), different lifecycle (fire-and-track vs synchronous), different resource limits, different failure modes. The QA skill explicitly requires: 'Reject boolean flag arguments immediately (split into two distinct functions).' The current shape means every caller, every test, and every reader must branch on a runtime flag to understand what the function returns.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Split into execute_bash_sync(cmd: &str) -> SyncResult and execute_bash_spawn(cmd: &str) -> Result<ShellId, _>.
2. At the tool-dispatch layer (mod.rs:809) expose these as two distinct tool names: 'bash' (synchronous) and 'bash_background' (returns shell_id). This matches the existing bash_output and kill_shell split.
3. The JSON schema at mod.rs:109-126 already lists run_in_background as a parameter — replace with a second tool entry. The model chooses the right one explicitly.
4. Downstream: remove the run_in_background field from the bash tool schema and the model will stop incorrectly mixing the two modes (current code silently swallows unknown arg values because unwrap_or(false) at line 318).
